Yolulo Operations
Promotions

Promotions & Coupons

How to create and manage all promotion types — Coupons (Product Discounts, Order Discounts, Delivery Fee Discounts, Buy X Get Y, Free Gifts), Popups, Push Notifications, and Highlight Merchants

Platform Hyperzod Admin
Section Promotions
Version 1.1
Updated March 2026
← Hub Overview Delivery Fee Buy X Get Y Free Gift Popups Push Notifications Highlight Common Settings
Promotions Overview

The Promotions Area

Navigate to Promotions in the left sidebar. The area has four tabs: Coupons, Popups, Push Notifications, and Highlight.

TAB 01
🎟️
Coupons
Create discount codes and automatic discounts across five types: Product, Order, Delivery Fee, Buy X Get Y, and Free Gift.
TAB 02
💬
Popups
Create image or text popups that appear to customers on web or mobile. Control frequency, location, and which segment sees them.
TAB 03
🔔
Push Notifications
Send instant or scheduled push notifications to all customers or specific segments. Link to Home or a specific Merchant.
TAB 04
⭐
Highlight Merchants
Mark individual merchants as Featured (appear in Featured Merchants section) and/or Sponsored (marked as sponsored in listings).
Coupon Type 3

Delivery Fee Discount

Reduce or waive the delivery fee using a coupon code or automatic discount. Supports percentage or fixed-amount off the delivery fee.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Coupons → + Create New → select Delivery Fee Discount
Usage Limits & Stacking
Limit total uses
Optional cap on how many times this coupon can be used across all customers. Enter a number when checked.
Limit uses per customer
Optional cap per individual customer. Enter a number when checked.
Combine with other discounts
Choose which other active discount types this can stack with: Product discounts and/or Order discounts. (No delivery fee stacking — you can't stack two delivery discounts.)
Delivery Fee Discount — usage limits, combine with other discounts, and active dates sections
Active Dates
Start Date Required
Date the coupon becomes active. Defaults to today.
Start Time
Time the coupon activates on the start date.
Set end date Optional
If unchecked the coupon runs indefinitely. Check to set an expiry date and time.
⚠️
Save: Click + Save in the top-right corner. The coupon will be active immediately if today's date is on or after the start date and Active Status is enabled.
Coupon Type 4

Buy X Get Y

Reward customers who buy a minimum quantity or spend a minimum amount from a merchant with discounted (or free) items from the same merchant.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Coupons → + Create New → select Buy X Get Y
Step 1 — Discount Type & Code
Discount Type Required
Coupon Code — customer must enter a code at checkout. Automatic Discount — applied automatically with no code needed.
Coupon Code If Coupon Code
Enter the code customers will use (e.g. BUY2GET1). Must be unique.
Description Optional
Internal note. Supports multiple languages via the language selector.
Public Coupon
When enabled, coupon is publicly discoverable. When disabled, only customers with the code can use it.
Active Status
Enabled by default. Uncheck to save as inactive/draft.
Buy X Get Y — Discount Type, Coupon Code, Discount Settings, and live summary panel
Step 2 — Buy X Get Y Configuration
Merchant Selection Required
Search and select one merchant. Both Customer Buys and Customer Gets will use products and categories from this merchant only.
Customer Buys — trigger type
Minimum quantity of items — customer must add at least N items. Minimum purchase amount — customer must spend at least £X.
Any items from
Restrict trigger items to a specific category, or leave as "Any category".
Select Products
Optionally narrow to specific products. Leave blank to apply to all products in the selected category.
Buy X Get Y Configuration — select merchant first, then configure Customer Buys trigger
Step 3 — Customer Gets
Quantity
How many free or discounted items the customer receives. Default is 1.
Any items from
Category the "get" items must come from (from the same merchant).
Select Products
Optionally narrow to specific products for the reward.
At a Discounted Value
Percentage — % off each reward item. Amount off each — fixed £ off each item. Free — reward items are completely free.
Customer Gets section — quantity, category/product selection, and discount value type
Step 4 — Eligible Customers & Order Types
Eligible Customers
All customers — any registered customer. Specific segments — target a customer segment. Specific customers — hand-pick individual accounts.
Order Types
Toggle All Order Types on (default) or off to select specific types (e.g. delivery only, pickup only).
Payment Methods
Toggle All Payment Methods on (default) or off to restrict to specific payment methods.
Eligible Customers section and Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods
Step 5 — Usage Limits, Stacking & Active Dates
Limit total uses
Optional cap on total coupon redemptions across all customers.
Limit uses per customer
Optional cap per individual customer account.
Combine with other discounts
Select which other discount types this can stack with: Product discounts, Order discounts, Delivery fee discounts.
Start Date Required
Date and time the coupon activates.
Set end date Optional
Leave unchecked for no expiry, or check to set an end date and time.
Usage limits, coupon stacking options, and Active Dates for Buy X Get Y
⚠️
Merchant must be selected first — the Customer Buys and Customer Gets sections remain greyed out until a merchant is chosen. Both sections draw products and categories from the same merchant.
Coupon Type 5

Free Gift

Give customers a specific free product from a merchant when they meet a minimum spend threshold.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Coupons → + Create New → select Free Gift
Step 1 — Discount Type & Code
Discount Type Required
Coupon Code — customer enters a code. Automatic Discount — applied automatically at checkout.
Coupon Code If Coupon Code
Unique code the customer enters (e.g. FREEGIFT50).
Description Optional
Internal note with optional multilingual support.
Public Coupon
Publicly discoverable when on. When off, only customers with the code can access it.
Active Status
Checked by default. Uncheck to save as a draft.
Free Gift — Discount Type, Coupon Code, Discount Settings, and live Discount Summary panel
Step 2 — Free Gift Configuration
Merchant Selection Required
Search and select the merchant. The free gift product must come from this merchant's catalogue.
Customer Buys — Minimum purchase amount
The minimum spend (£) required to trigger the free gift. Example: £50. Configured after selecting a merchant.
Customer Gets — Select Free Gift Product Required
Search and select the specific product to give as the free gift. Must be from the selected merchant.
Free Gift Configuration — merchant selection, minimum purchase amount, and free gift product selection
Step 3 — Eligible Customers & Order Types
Eligible Customers
All customers, Specific segments, or Specific customers.
Order Types
All Order Types enabled by default. Disable to select specific types.
Payment Methods
All Payment Methods enabled by default. Disable to restrict to specific methods.
Eligible Customers, Eligible Order Types & Payment Methods for Free Gift
Step 4 — Usage Limits, Stacking & Active Dates
Limit total uses
Optional total redemption cap across all customers.
Limit uses per customer
Optional cap per individual customer.
Combine with other discounts
Stack with Product discounts, Order discounts, and/or Delivery fee discounts.
Start Date Required
Date and time the free gift coupon activates.
Set end date Optional
Leave unchecked for no expiry, or set an end date and time.
Free Gift — usage limits, coupon stacking, and Active Dates. The Discount Summary panel on the right reflects all saved values live.
🎁
Discount Summary panel — the right-hand panel updates in real time as you fill in the form. It shows: Discount Type, Coupon Code, Discount Value, Active Period, Min Requirements, Eligible Customers, Usage Limits, and Free Gift rule (e.g. "Spend £50, get 1 free gift"). Use this as a final sanity-check before saving.
Promotions Tab 2

Popups

Create image-based or text-based popup banners that appear to customers on web and/or mobile. Control which customer segment sees them, how often, and where they appear.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Popups tab → + Create New
Popup List View
Columns shown
Title · Type · Location · Visible On · Frequency · Status (toggle) · Actions (edit / delete)
Status toggle
Blue = active (popup is live). Grey = inactive (popup is paused). Toggle directly from the list without entering edit mode.
Popups list — a popup with Status toggled ON (blue). Columns show type, location, visible on, and frequency.
Same popup with Status toggled OFF (grey/inactive). Toggle changes take effect immediately.
Popup Form Fields — Left Column
Title Required
Internal name for the popup (e.g. Feedback!). Shown in the list but not necessarily to customers.
Status Required
Active — popup is live. Inactive — popup is saved but not shown to customers.
Location Required
Global — appears across the whole platform. Other options may include specific pages.
Action Type
Link — tapping the popup opens a URL. Other types may include navigation actions.
Action Value
The URL or value for the action type (e.g. https://tally.so/r/J9IA04).
Type Required
Image — upload an image file. Other types may support text or HTML content.
Pop-up size
Width: 400px, Height up to 800px. Click Upload to attach the image file.
Popup Form Fields — Right Column
Customer Segmentation
All Customers — shown to every customer. Specific Customer Segment — restrict to a defined segment.
Frequency Required
Show everytime — popup appears every time the customer visits. Once — popup is shown only once per customer.
Visible On
Both — visible on web and mobile app. Can restrict to web only or mobile only. If not set, defaults to both.
Edit Popup — left column fields (title, status, location, action type/value, type, upload) and right column (customer segmentation, frequency, visible on). Device preview updates live.
After uploading an image, it appears in the editor. The device preview on the right shows how the popup will look on a mobile screen.
📱
Device preview — the right panel renders a live mobile preview of the popup. It provides a general idea of appearance; actual rendering varies by device. Always test on a real device for final confirmation.
Promotions Tab 3

Push Notifications

Send targeted push notifications to customers. Choose instant delivery or schedule for later, target all users or a specific segment, and link to Home or a specific merchant.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Push Notifications tab → + Create
Notification Campaigns List
Columns shown
Campaign (name) · Date Created · Status · Actions
Search
Search Campaigns box filters by campaign name.
Notification Campaigns list. "+ Create" button (top right) opens the Create Notification form.
Step 1 — Notification Content
Notification title Required
The bold headline of the push notification (shown on device lock screen and notification tray). Supports emoji via the emoji picker (😊).
Notification body Required
The body text of the notification (appears beneath the title). Supports emoji. Keep concise — long text may be truncated by the OS.
Step 2 — Schedule
Schedule type
Instant — sends immediately on save. Other options may include scheduled date/time.
Create Notification — Step 1 (Notification title & body) and Step 2 (Schedule: Instant)
Step 3 — Target Audience
Select a segment
All Users — sends to every customer with push notifications enabled. Specific Segment — restrict to a defined customer segment.
Step 4 — Navigation
Navigates To
Home — tapping the notification opens the app home screen. Merchant — tapping opens a specific merchant page (select the merchant after choosing this option).
Step 3: Target Audience (All Users or Specific Segment) and Step 4: Navigation (Home or Merchant)
Device Preview
Preview
A live mobile device preview shows how the notification will appear on a customer's lock screen. This is indicative only — actual rendering varies by device and OS.
Device Preview section — shows a simulated mobile lock screen. Test on a real device for actual results.
⚠️
Instant sends are immediate — clicking + Save with Schedule set to Instant will dispatch the notification straight away to all targeted users. Double-check the title, body, audience, and navigation before saving.
Promotions Tab 4

Highlight Merchants

Control which merchants appear in Featured and Sponsored placements across the customer-facing storefront. Each merchant can be individually configured.

💡
Navigation: Promotions → Highlight tab
Highlight Merchants List
Columns shown
Merchant logo · Name & Address · Promotion Type · Action (edit icon)
Search
Search Merchants box filters the list in real time.
Promotion Type column
Shows the current highlight status for each merchant. A dash (–) means no promotion is set.
Action button
Click the pencil/edit icon (✎) on any merchant row to open the Configure Highlight modal.
Highlight Merchants list — all merchants shown with their current Promotion Type and an edit action per row
Configure Highlight Modal
Featured toggle
When ON — merchant appears in the Featured Merchants section of the storefront. Status pills at the top of the modal show Featured: ON / OFF.
Sponsored toggle
When ON — merchant is marked as Sponsored in listings. Status pills show Sponsored: ON / OFF.
Validation
At least one toggle must be enabled to save. If both are off, the "Enable at least one promotion" message appears and the Save button remains disabled.
Save
Click Save to apply. Click Cancel to discard changes.
Configure Highlight modal — Featured and Sponsored toggles with live status pills. Both are OFF by default; at least one must be enabled to save.
⭐
Featured vs Sponsored: Featured places the merchant in a dedicated "Featured Merchants" carousel/section. Sponsored adds a "Sponsored" label to the merchant's regular listing card. Both can be active at the same time on the same merchant.
Reference

Common Coupon Settings

These settings appear on every coupon type. Use this as a quick-reference guide.

Discount Type Options
Coupon Code
Customer must manually enter a code at checkout. Requires a unique Coupon Code field entry.
Automatic Discount
Applied automatically — no code needed. Good for site-wide sales or targeted segment promotions.
Discount Settings
Public Coupon
ON — coupon is publicly listed and discoverable. OFF — only customers who already have the code can use it.
Active Status
ON — coupon is usable. OFF — coupon is saved but cannot be redeemed. Useful for drafts.
Eligible Customers
All customers
Any registered customer can use this coupon.
Specific segments
Only customers who belong to the chosen customer segment(s).
Specific customers
Hand-picked individual customer accounts only.
Coupon Type Stacks with Product Stacks with Order Stacks with Delivery
Product Discount—✓ Optional✓ Optional
Order Discount✓ Optional—✓ Optional
Delivery Fee Discount✓ Optional✓ Optional—
Buy X Get Y✓ Optional✓ Optional✓ Optional
Free Gift✓ Optional✓ Optional✓ Optional
ℹ️
Discount Summary panel — every coupon creation form includes a live Discount Summary panel on the right side. It updates in real time as you fill in each field and serves as a final sanity check before clicking + Save.
Yolulo
